cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the answer to this question. The question is asking about the duration of the experiment mentioned in the text. The options are A) about three days, B) about ten days, C) about two weeks, or D) about a month. First, I need to go through the text and find information about the timeline of the experiment. Let me read through the text again to make sure I don't miss any details. The text mentions that participants were asked to write for 20 minutes per day for three consecutive days. Then, two weeks after the third day of writing, all participants received a skin biopsy. The wounds were photographed regularly over the following days. On the 11th day after the biopsy, some wounds had completely healed. So, the writing part of the experiment lasted three days. Then, there was a two-week waiting period before the biopsy was done. After the biopsy, they monitored the healing process for 11 days. The question is about the duration of the experiment overall. Wait, but when they say "experiment" here, does that refer to the entire study including the writing phase, the waiting period, and the monitoring of the wounds? Let me check the text again. The experiment included the writing sessions (three days), followed by a two-week wait, then the biopsy and 11 days of monitoring. Let's calculate the total time. Three days of writing, then two weeks (which is 14 days) of waiting, then 11 days of monitoring. That totals 3 + 14 + 11 = 28
